posts.text: Two oil tankers and two LPG carriers are crossing the Strait of Hormuz **inbound** via the Omani route with their AIS turned on (under heavy US Navy / Airforce watch). Washington trying hard to keep the Omani route on.
posts.summary:
# Oil Market Summary

The U.S. is actively monitoring shipping traffic through an alternative Omani route around the Strait of Hormuz to maintain crude oil and LPG supply flows, signaling efforts to sustain export channels amid regional tensions.

posts.text:
Iran’s foreign affairs minister:

“… Any attempt to adopt new or separate arrangements compared to what is underway by the Islamic Republic of Iran, will only lead to more complicated situations and delays in the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, and will increase the tensions,
posts.summary: Iran's foreign minister warned that deviating from current arrangements regarding the Strait of Hormuz could complicate reopening efforts and escalate tensions, signaling potential risks to one of the world's most critical oil chokepoints. The statement implies Iran may restrict or delay normalizing shipping through the strait if its preferred terms are not accepted.

posts.summary: Global oil and gas producers must invest over $500 billion annually to maintain current output levels as existing fields naturally decline, according to the International Energy Agency. This substantial maintenance investment requirement has direct implications for oil supply stability and production costs in the market.

posts.text:
Global energy spending is on track to reach $3.4 trillion in 2026

Around $2.2 trillion of this is set to go to grids, storage, low-emissions fuels, nuclear, renewables, efficiency & electrification

And about $1.2 trillion goes to oil, natural gas & coal: https://t.co/8IajZj1gIL https://t.co/yLRuHSwqq9
posts.summary: Global energy spending is projected to reach $3.4 trillion in 2026, with approximately $1.2 trillion allocated to fossil fuels including oil, natural gas, and coal. This represents a significant shift in energy investment priorities, as the majority of spending ($2.2 trillion) is directed toward clean energy infrastructure and technologies.

posts.summary: OPEC highlighted oil's role in enabling modern sports through synthetic materials, stadium infrastructure, and transportation fuels. The post underscores petroleum's broad industrial applications beyond energy production.

posts.text:
A huge surplus in global oil markets going into the Hormuz crisis provided a major cushion against the initial shock

But with global oil inventories falling at a record pace since then, the recovery in flows through the Strait has come at a critical time: https://t.co/vvjkiIlFD9 https://t.co/fUbLuwuQLl
posts.summary: The global oil market benefited from surplus inventory that cushioned the initial impact of the Hormuz crisis, but the subsequent rapid depletion of these reserves means that restored oil flows through the strait are now critically important to prevent supply tightness.

posts.text: Iran FM warns any challenge to Tehran’s control of Hormuz will ‘increase tensions’ https://t.co/aUjTSOHeUP
posts.summary: Iran's Foreign Minister has warned that any challenge to Tehran's control of the Strait of Hormuz could heighten tensions, a statement with potential implications for global oil supplies given the waterway's critical role in energy transportation. The warning suggests geopolitical risk to one of the world's most strategically important chokepoints for oil transit.

posts.text:
Ukraine hit two Russian oil refineries in the regions of Krasnodar ​and Yaroslavl overnight, President Volodymyr Zelenskiy said on ‌Sunday, as Kyiv ramps up pressure on Russia's fuel supply with its drone fleet.

Kyiv's increasingly frequent drone attacks have caused acute ​fuel
posts.summary: Ukraine conducted drone strikes on Russian oil refineries in Krasnodar and Yaroslavl, part of an escalating campaign to disrupt Russia's fuel supply. The increasingly frequent attacks are creating acute fuel shortages, with potential implications for global oil markets given Russia's role as a major energy producer.
